steps.title: Identify the tense of the dependent clause
steps.content: "When we arrived in Venice, Utah" is a simple past dependent clause. The main clause verb must match this tense — both actions (arriving and noticing the glyph) happened at the same completed moment in the past.

steps.title: Apply verb tense consistency — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
All verbs in the same narrative sequence must share the same tense unless there is a specific reason to shift.
  • Choice A — "notice": simple present. Breaks tense consistency with the past-tense dependent clause. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "are noticing": present progressive. Also present tense; conflicts with the past setting established by "arrived." Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "will notice": simple future. Cannot describe something that happened simultaneously with a past event. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "noticed": simple past. Matches "arrived" in tense, correctly showing both actions occurred together in the past. Correct.
